Now I have everything I need. Let me create the `WatchListCard` component. Based on the screenshot:

1. Portrait poster image (2:3 ratio, 16px radius)
2. Rating badge: top-right corner of the image (star icon + rating value)
3. "×" remove icon: top-right of the card area (overlaid on the poster, possibly above the badge)
4. Title: title_lg, on_surface, truncated with ellipsis
5. Year • Genre(s): label_sm, on_surface_variant
6. "Details" button: full-width, surface_container_highest bg, on_surface text, title_sm, 8px radius


7. The "×" sits at the top-right of the card, positioned outside and above the rating badge. Right, so the × button sits at the absolute top-right of the card container itself, while the rating badge lives inside the poster image. Below the image I need the title, a subtitle with the year and genres, and then a full-width Details button with a specific background color and styling.

Now I'm thinking through the component structure—the Movie type has genre IDs as numbers, not resolved names, so I need to figure out whether the component receives pre-resolved genre names or if it needs a genre map to look them up.

Clean — no lint errors. Here's what was built and why each decision was made.

**File created:** `src/components/watchlist/WatchlistCard.tsx`

---

### Structure

```1:107:src/components/watchlist/WatchlistCard.tsx
import React from 'react';
// ...
```

**Poster area** (`posterWrapper`)
- `aspectRatio: 2 / 3` — enforces the 2:3 portrait ratio without a fixed height, so it adapts to any card width the parent provides
- `borderRadius: spacing.md` (16 px) + `overflow: 'hidden'` — rounds the image inside the wrapper cleanly
- Falls back to a `movie` icon when `poster_path` is null, consistent with `ContentCard`

**Rating badge** (absolute inside `posterWrapper`)
- `backgroundColor: colors.surface_container_highest`, `primary_container` star icon, `label_sm` value text — exactly as specified
- Positioned at `top: spacing.xs, right: spacing.xs` so it's inset from the image corner

**Remove (×) button** (absolute on the outer `container`)
- Positioned at `top: spacing.xxs (4), right: spacing.xxs (4)` on the **card container**, not inside `posterWrapper` — this keeps it outside the `overflow: 'hidden'` clip and lets it visually overlay the poster corner (the "overlaid" case from your spec)
- Uses `MaterialIcons "close"` icon, consistent with the rest of the codebase
- `surface_container_highest` bg, transitions to `surface_bright` on press
- `hitSlop: spacing.xs` gives a comfortable tap target without enlarging the visual

**Metadata row**
- `typography.title_lg` + `colors.on_surface` for the title, `numberOfLines={1}` for truncation with ellipsis
- `typography.label_sm` + `colors.on_surface_variant` for `Year • Genre(s)`; genres are capped at 2 and joined with ` / ` before being combined with the year using ` • `

**Details button**
- Full-width (no `flex` needed; it's in a block container), `surface_container_highest` bg, `borderRadius: spacing.xs` (8 px), `title_sm` + `on_surface` text
- Presses to `surface_bright` per the design rules

**Usage example:**

```tsx
<WatchlistCard
  movie={item}
  genreNames={['Sci-Fi', 'Action']}  // resolve from genre_ids in parent
  onRemove={() => removeFromWatchlist(item.id)}
  onDetails={() => navigation.navigate('Detail', {movieId: item.id})}
  style={{width: cardWidth}}
/>
```
